Who I Am

About Me

Hi, I'm Abbas Alhimed. I was born in Iraq and came to the United States at the age of 7 as a refugee. That journey taught me early on that hard work and education are the most powerful tools you can have, and I've carried that mindset into everything I do.

Today I'm a freshman at the University of Texas at Dallas, carrying junior-level credits and a 4.0 GPA in Computer Information Systems and Technology. I completed dual credit coursework at Dallas College while still in high school, which gave me a head start and a deep appreciation for learning.

What drew me to data analytics is simple: I love how data can be used to solve real world business problems. That passion led me to pursue a Business Analyst internship at Nutrify AI, where I analyzed customer data, built workflow documentation, and contributed to product roadmap discussions.

I also serve on the board of the Arab Student Association at UTD, where I help organize cultural events and build community for Arab students on campus.

I'm also self teaching Power BI on the side because I believe the best data analysts don't wait to be taught they go and learn.

As a first generation college student, I carry something into every room that most people don't think about — I don't have a roadmap. Every step I take is one my family hasn't taken before me, and that pushes me to be determined, focused, and always striving for more.

At the end of the day, I work hard because I want a future I'm genuinely happy in and because I want to make my family proud. That's what drives me.

Daily Habit

Running

I like it because it is a great way to challenge myself while being active. Running pushes me mentally and physically every mile is a small win that adds up over time. Whether it's an early morning easy run or a tough tempo session, getting out there consistently has become a core part of who I am.

I log every run and am always chasing the next personal best. Tracking my data keeps me accountable and motivated seeing the numbers improve over weeks makes all the hard work worth it.

Good Food, Good Company

Eating Out

I love trying new things with friends. Some of the best experiences I have had are just going somewhere none of us have been before, ordering things we have not tried, and seeing what happens.

Growing up in an Iraqi household, food was always a big deal. Sharing a meal is how you build real connections with people.

What I Bring

Skills & Projects

Technical Toolkit

📗
Microsoft Excel
My most used tool. Pivot tables, VLOOKUP, SUMIF, and charts that turn numbers into clear visuals. Where I first learned to think analytically.
🗄️
SQL
SELECT statements, WHERE filters, JOIN across multiple tables, and GROUP BY aggregations. Building depth through coursework and independent practice on real datasets.
📊
Power BI
Self taught. I connect data sources, clean with Power Query, build interactive dashboards, and write DAX formulas to create calculated measures.
📋
Data Analysis
Identifying patterns and trends to support business decisions. Applied directly during my internship at Nutrify AI.
🧠
C++ / JavaScript
Beginner level programming actively developed through UTD coursework.
🌐
Arabic
Native fluency alongside English. A valuable skill in diverse professional environments.

Academic & Personal Work

🎯
Class Project · 2026
The Unspoken Rules: Team Culture & Authenticity Framework
A collaborative class project on interpersonal dynamics and team culture, where I researched and presented two core frameworks: The 3 C's (Commitment, Compatibility, Competence) and Authenticity Zones (Sacred, Negotiable, and Indifferent).
The 3 C's Framework: Commitment (excited to learn and grow), Compatibility (works well with team), Competence (capable of being here) establishing core expectations for team culture
Authenticity Zones: Sacred Zone (reject the rules), Negotiable Zone (bend the rules), Indifferent Zone (embrace the rules) defining how individuals approach cultural norms
Researched interpersonal dynamics and team behavior concepts, then presented findings to the full group with visual frameworks for practical application
Group · Spring 2026ResearchPresentationTeam DynamicsCommunication
🤖
ITSS 4330 · Systems Analysis & Design
MochiThings: AI-Powered Product Discovery & Personalization Engine
Full feasibility analysis for an AI personalization system for MochiThings, a stationery e-commerce retailer.
System Request with business needs and constraints for sponsor Leon Yip
Functional and Technical Feasibility classified high-risk due to AI complexity
Economic analysis: $853,500 costs, $280K annual benefits, ~17.5% ROI, break-even 2029
Group · Spring 2026Feasibility AnalysisEconomic AnalysisAI/ML

BCOM · Business Communication
Professional Email Revision & Workplace Communication
Reviewed and professionally revised a poorly written internal email. Applied real world business writing, tone management, and professional communication skills.
Proofreading feedback using Track Changes and Comments in Word
Full email rewrite restructured message, preserved intent
Professional reply email graded on workplace-readiness rubric

Personal · Data Analytics
E-Commerce Sales Analysis Dashboard | Excel
Built a fully interactive Excel dashboard analyzing e-commerce sales data across sales, profit, quantity, orders, and profit margin with year-over-year growth tracking.
KPI cards with conditional formatting showing Sales $2.3M, Profit $286K, Orders 9,994, and YoY growth percentages
Trend analysis using monthly sales and profit line and column charts side by side
Waterfall chart for category-wise profit breakdown and donut chart for sales share by category
Map visualization showing sales distribution by state and Top 10 Sub-Categories bar chart
Interactive slicers for Year, Region, and Segment for dynamic drill-down filtering

KPI cards showing Total Sales $1.57M, Profit $175K, Quantity 22K, and Cities 6K
Regional donut charts for Sales by Region, Segment, and Payment Mode
Monthly YOY profit and sales trend lines comparing 2019 vs 2020 performance

Personal · Data Tracking
Running Training Log | Excel Spreadsheet
A personal Excel based running training log tracking every run from February to March 2026, built to practice real data tracking and spreadsheet skills outside the classroom. I like it because it is a great way to challenge myself while being active. I log every run and am always chasing the next personal best.
59 day log tracking distance, pace, heart rate, calories, weather, and run type per session
Monthly summary using Excel formulas: total miles, average pace, max distance, total calories
Color coded run types: Easy Run, Tempo, Long Run, Recovery, Intervals, Hill Run
Weekly Goal vs Actual line chart built directly in Excel tracking mileage across the 8 week block
